Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Boomloom: Think with your hands 142 upvotes, 14 comments
  2. My first patch to the Linux kernel 140 upvotes, 18 comments
  3. Sashiko: An agentic Linux kernel code review system 36 upvotes, 3 comments
  4. Electronics for Kids, 2nd Edition 212 upvotes, 46 comments
  5. Bayesian statistics for confused data scientists 144 upvotes, 37 comments
  6. Some things just take time 750 upvotes, 239 comments
  7. Floci – A free, open-source local AWS emulator 222 upvotes, 65 comments
  8. Professional video editing, right in the browser with WebGPU and WASM 299 upvotes, 103 comments
  9. Grafeo – A fast, lean, embeddable graph database built in Rust 232 upvotes, 79 comments
  10. 25 Years of Eggs 84 upvotes, 26 comments
  11. Linking Smaller Haskell Binaries (2023) 16 upvotes, 2 comments
  12. Hide macOS Tahoe's Menu Icons 237 upvotes, 93 comments
  13. Flash-Moe: Running a 397B Parameter Model on a Mac with 48GB RAM 62 upvotes, 19 comments
  14. Hormuz Minesweeper – Are you tired of winning? 331 upvotes, 147 comments
  15. Node.js worker threads are problematic, but they work great for us 11 upvotes, 1 comments
  16. Do Not Turn Child Protection into Internet Access Control 744 upvotes, 380 comments
  17. It's Their Mona Lisa 44 upvotes, 15 comments
  18. HopTab–free,open source macOS app switcher and tiler that replaces Cmd+Tab 48 upvotes, 18 comments
  19. Tinybox – A powerful computer for deep learning 523 upvotes, 295 comments
  20. 'Miracle': Europe reconnects with lost spacecraft 45 upvotes, 17 comments
  21. The three pillars of JavaScript bloat 365 upvotes, 203 comments
  22. Windows native app development is a mess 24 upvotes, 7 comments
  23. Thinking Fast, Slow, and Artificial: How AI Is Reshaping Human Reasoning 169 upvotes, 96 comments
  24. Show HN: Oku – One tab to filter out noise from feeds and content sources 11 upvotes, 2 comments
  25. Chest Fridge (2009) 134 upvotes, 76 comments
  26. More common mistakes to avoid when creating system architecture diagrams 8 upvotes, 1 comments
  27. Show HN: Time Keep – Location timezones, timers, alarms, countdowns in one place 17 upvotes, 6 comments
  28. How We Synchronized Editing for Rec Room's Multiplayer Scripting System 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  29. Cross-Model Void Convergence: GPT-5.2 and Claude Opus 4.6 Deterministic Silence 37 upvotes, 21 comments
  30. Vatican Rebukes Peter Thiel's Antichrist Lectures in Rome 35 upvotes, 21 comments

Last generated 9:00pm, 22 March 2026. Source here.
